text:
I'll search for all items simultaneously to check their existence.
Both searches returned strong, unambiguous results. Here is the analysis and final verdict:

---

**ITEM 0 — "On semiamarts, amarts, and processes with finite value"**


This paper by Ulrich Krengel and Louis Sucheston appears in *Probability on Banach Spaces*, published by Dekker, New York, 1978, pp. 197–266.
 It is widely cited across multiple academic sources including AMS, SpringerLink, arXiv preprints, and Dagstuhl proceedings. All metadata in the citation (authors, venue, year) match exactly.

---

**ITEM 1 — "Language models are unsupervised multitask learners"**


This paper's authors are Alec Radford, Jeffrey Wu, Rewon Child, David Luan, Dario Amodei, and Ilya Sutskever.
 
The official code repository on GitHub is hosted at openai/gpt-2.
 
It was published in the OpenAI blog in 2019.
 The cited metadata (authors, venue, year) all match perfectly.
